categories.summary: The main discussion centered on elizaOS v2.0.0 release announcement and token economics clarification. A new version (v2.0.0) was released, with users inquiring about release notes and features. The most significant technical discussion involved mbat8088 asking detailed questions about $elizaOS token utility and economics. Key questions covered: legal structure relationship between Milady and elizaOS, buyback mechanisms from product profits, potential Milady token plans, buyback percentage targets, and governance structure. odilitime provided authoritative answers clarifying that elizaOS did not launch Milady but built on their stack using fees, Milady has separate cloud infrastructure for its own buybacks, no additional tokens planned except possibly Babylon in distant future, no formal buyback percentage set as focus remains on achieving profitability first, and no formal governance structure currently exists. The discussion revealed the project is in early stages focusing on product delivery (elizaOS framework, Milady app, Eliza Cloud) before finalizing tokenomics details. Two developers (keil0780 and rsn6958) posted availability for collaboration on AI and full-stack projects. A scammer was called out by mbat8088. Reference was made to v3 launch being close and previous post by Odi in another channel from May 3rd.

faqs.question: What is the $elizaOS token utility?
What's new in v2? Cannot find release notes
Are Milady and elizaOS the same legal entity?
Does product profits buyback include Milady revenue or only Eliza Cloud revenue?
Is there a separate Milady token planned?
Is there a specific buyback percentage target?
Will buyback percentage be set through formal governance or team discretion?

helpInteractions.context: mbat8088 asked about elizaOS token utility and was directed to information
helpInteractions.resolution: Pointed to post from Odi in another channel from May 3rd

helpInteractions.context: Detailed questions about token economics, legal structure, buybacks, and governance
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided comprehensive answers clarifying Milady relationship, no new tokens planned, no formal buyback percentage yet, and no formal governance structure

actionItems.description: Publish v2.0.0 release notes with feature details
Formalize and document token buyback percentage and governance mechanisms
Focus on achieving profitability with product stack before finalizing tokenomics

categories.summary: This Discord channel segment shows early-stage community introductions and networking. based.bid announced their recently published SDK and expressed interest in integrating with ElizaOS, seeking to understand what community members are building. odilitime responded that they are working on trading bots. Two full-stack developers (keil0780 and trace.g) posted detailed introductions offering their services. keil0780 specializes in React, Node.js, Python, OpenAI API, and PyTorch, focusing on AI-integrated applications and workflow automation. trace.g positions themselves as an AI + Full Stack Engineer specializing in LLM systems, agents, RAG, multimodal AI, and automation, emphasizing their ability to transition projects from manual workflows and prototypes to production-ready AI systems with clean orchestration and scalable backend flows. Both developers are actively seeking freelance, contract, or collaboration opportunities. No technical problems were solved, no specific implementations were discussed, and no concrete decisions were made during this segment. The conversation represents typical community networking and service offerings rather than active development discussion.

faqs.question: What is everyone here working on?

categories.summary: The discussion centered on significant technical achievements with ElizaOS. zadayos successfully booted ElizaOS Debian RISC-V 64-bit ISO on QEMU chip emulator in 35 seconds, demonstrating cross-architecture compatibility. shawmakesmagic confirmed Eliza v3 is running on elizaOS (Linux) on an emulated RISC-V chip called 'eliza e1', representing a major milestone in hardware abstraction. When asked about custom chip development, the question about a physical Eliza E1 chip remained unanswered. On the business side, shawmakesmagic clarified that the previously mentioned Babylon airdrop for elizaos holders will not happen due to budget constraints, with the team now focusing exclusively on elizaOS development. This strategic pivot was strongly supported by zadayos, who advocated for single-token focus similar to ETH/SOL utility models, leaving ecosystem token creation to the market. Regarding Milady token buyback, odilitime confirmed it's the BNB version. janabr8973 announced a new DeFi plugin for Eliza on Base/Aave, offering real-time solvency scoring and flash-crash simulations via NPM package @centinel-risk-protocol/eliza-plugin-centinel, seeking community testing and feedback.

faqs.question: Do you intend to make your own chip (Eliza E1) for Elizaos in the future?
Which milady token buyback with milady app revenue? It's on BNB or on Solana?
Is the CA on coingecko correct for bnb/base/eth?
Any update for degenai?
I remember an airdrop to elizaos holders was mentioned long ago did it happen?

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about which Milady token (BNB or Solana) is used for buyback with Milady app revenue
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed it's the BNB version of the token

actionItems.description: Test and provide feedback on Centinel Risk Protocol Eliza plugin for DeFi agents on Base/Aave with real-time solvency scoring and flash-crash simulations
Focus exclusively on elizaOS development and create multiple utility token uses similar to ETH or SOL model

categories.summary: The channel discussion centered on dockerizing a VoiceAI white label agency platform for local deployment. greggblazer, a founder, sought advice on containerization challenges. keil0780 provided detailed technical guidance, identifying key pain points: real-time voice dependencies, GPU access, networking, persistent storage, and latency stability across environments. The recommended approach was to separate services (inference, API, database, queue/worker) and containerize gradually rather than attempting a monolithic migration. greggblazer confirmed progress to two working containers but expressed the goal of achieving single-container deployment for mass deployment ease. The conversation highlighted the challenge of merging frontend, backend, and database containers into one unified container. keil0780 later introduced themselves as a full-stack AI developer interested in AI integrations, workflow automation, SaaS platforms, backend systems, AI agents, and productivity applications.

faqs.question: Any advice on dockerizing a VoiceAI white label agency platform so it can go local?
Any experience in merging front/backend to database in containers into one?

helpInteractions.context: Dockerizing VoiceAI platform for local deployment with challenges around real-time voice, GPU access, and latency
helpInteractions.resolution: Advised separating services (inference, API, database, queue/worker) and containerizing gradually instead of monolithic approach

actionItems.description: Merge two working containers (frontend/backend and database) into single container for mass deployment
Handle real-time voice dependencies, GPU access, networking, and persistent storage in containerized environment

categories.summary: The channel featured several community introductions and a major product launch. rsn6958 introduced themselves as an experienced Web3 developer offering support for blockchain development, smart contracts, Solidity, and dApp integrations. keil0780 introduced themselves as a full-stack and AI developer focused on AI integrations, workflow automation, SaaS platforms, backend systems, AI agents, dashboards, and productivity applications. The main technical highlight was shawmakesmagic's announcement of waifu.fun going live - an agentic launchpad built using elizaOS v3 agents. The platform was developed by an agent named Sol (The Architect) and is fully open source with code available on GitHub. waifu.fun represents a novel approach to agent-powered launchpads in the Web3 space. issaarts offered digital art and animation services to the community. The discussion was primarily introductory with the waifu.fun launch being the only concrete technical development shared.

actionItems.description: waifu.fun agentic launchpad launched - built with elizaOS v3 agents, open source platform for agent-powered launches

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains minimal technical discussion. The primary substantive exchange involved babyshark0x inquiring about purchasing ElizaOS tokens and migration options. shawmakesmagic confirmed that the highest liquidity for ElizaOS is on BSC (Binance Smart Chain) and clarified that the migration period from ai16z to ElizaOS has ended, with the migration team having moved on. A researcher (blublue3781) posted seeking interviews with developers who have built AI agents with wallet access to discuss security implementations including spending caps and prompt injection protection. dannynor asked about ElizaOS roadmap for the coming months but received no response. keil0780 posted a lengthy self-introduction as a freelance senior engineer with AI and full-stack experience. The remaining messages were brief greetings, spam reports, and casual check-ins with no technical content.

faqs.question: Where do we buy ElizaOS? On BSC? Can people still swap ai16z for ElizaOS?
What does the next few months look like for ElizaOS?

helpInteractions.context: User needed information about where to purchase ElizaOS tokens and whether ai16z migration was still available
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed BSC has most liquidity and clarified that migration period has ended with the migration team having moved on

actionItems.description: Provide updated roadmap and plans for ElizaOS for the next few months

categories.summary: The discussion centered on the announcement of Eliza's launch of the first economic agent launchpad called 'waifu'. satsbased shared that Sol the architect and an Eliza agent were the primary builders of this launchpad platform. The announcement was accompanied by a link to a Twitter/X post providing more details about the launch. This represents a significant milestone in economic agent infrastructure, combining AI agent capabilities with a launchpad mechanism for deployment.

faqs.question: What did Eliza launch?

actionItems.description: Economic agent launchpad 'waifu' launched by Eliza, built by Sol the architect and Eliza agent

categories.summary: This Discord chat segment contains primarily introductory messages and self-promotions rather than technical discussions. The only substantive technical interaction involves .maxlife encountering an error while manually launching on waifu and attempting to upload to flap. The error message indicates a 400 status with 'Operations and map are required' when using flap upload. The user mentioned retrying multiple times without success. No resolution or detailed troubleshooting was provided in this segment. The remaining messages consist of: rsn6958 introducing themselves as an AI and full stack developer with experience in RAG systems, workflow automation, and SaaS platforms; sindy.eth recruiting for an onchain game project seeking testers, moderators, and Solidity developers; itssowenn4462 presenting their background in AI/ML and full stack engineering with focus on autonomous systems, RAG pipelines, voice AI, and computer vision. Their tech stack includes React, Next.js, Node.js, Python, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, OpenAI, LangChain, and AWS services. Other messages were brief greetings or requests (dexploarer sharing a link, stackvector mentioning a proposal submission, .tutak greeting the channel, and .maxlife requesting an invite code). No architectural decisions, implementation details, or problem resolutions were discussed in depth during this segment.
